endpoints.description: Academia profile works API. Use it after identifying a profile user_id; browser_api_call compacts paper titles, years, abstracts, and automatically joins public view counts so the agent can rank top viewed works without scrolling or opening paper pages.
strategy_notes: Extract user_id from the Academia profile page once, for example data-follow-user-id on the Follow button. For Yoshua Bengio, the observed profile user_id is 32808396.

Call this endpoint with per_page=50&offset=0 to fetch the visible profile works in one batch. The runtime automatically calls the public works/views API for the returned ids and merges view counts into the compact result.
Rank by the compact result's views field and answer from the top_by_views summary. Do not scroll the profile or open individual paper detail pages just to find views or abstracts.
If publication_date.year is missing from the compact metadata, record the year as Not shown instead of doing extra detail-page navigation.

endpoints.description: Academia public work view-count API. Usually called automatically by the profile works runtime join; only call directly when view counts are needed for an already known set of work ids.
strategy_notes: Prefer the profile works endpoint first because it returns titles, publication years, and abstracts; this endpoint only returns id-to-view-count mappings.
Batch all known work ids in a single URL where possible.
